description: Fix four resource leak and test isolation bugs in E2E tests where cleanup code is missing or unreachable on failure paths. After all stories pass: (1) the WebSocket in security.e2e.ts is always closed in a finally block; (2) the strict-CSP server's connect-src state is always reset after each test, preventing isolation failures; (3) the side-panel-open-tab.e2e.ts homepageServer is properly closed and its EADDRINUSE fallback is corrected; (4) the stress-mcp-dispatch.e2e.ts second page and progress.e2e.ts slow-mode state are always cleaned up on failure paths.

userStories.title: Move WebSocket close into finally block in security.e2e.ts
userStories.description: In security.e2e.ts, the raw WebSocket `ws` created for the concurrent-writes test (around line 700) is closed at line ~773 with `ws.close()`, which is placed just before the `finally` block. Any assertion failure in lines 738-771 (Promise.all results check, waitFor poll, or explicit field assertions) skips `ws.close()` and the connection leaks for the lifetime of the test process. The fix is to move `ws.close()` into the existing `finally` block alongside `server.kill()`.
acceptanceCriteria: `ws.close()` appears inside the `finally` block in the concurrent-writes test in e2e/security.e2e.ts
`ws.close()` is called even when any assertion between the WebSocket creation and the end of the try body throws

userStories.title: Reset connect-src server state in finally block in strict-csp.e2e.ts
userStories.description: In strict-csp.e2e.ts, one test calls `strictCspServer.control('set-connect-src', { connectSrcNone: true })` to test adapter injection when the page's connect-src CSP is 'none'. This state change is never reset in a finally block. If the test fails after this call (e.g., `openTestAppTab` throws), subsequent tests that share the `strictCspServer` fixture run against a server that blocks all connections, causing cascading failures that are hard to diagnose.
acceptanceCriteria: The `connect-src none` test in e2e/strict-csp.e2e.ts wraps its test body in a try/finally that calls `strictCspServer.control('set-connect-src', { connectSrcNone: false })` before the page is closed
The reset is in a finally block so it runs even if the test throws

userStories.title: Fix homepageServer cleanup and EADDRINUSE fallback in side-panel-open-tab.e2e.ts
userStories.description: In side-panel-open-tab.e2e.ts, the test that verifies the side panel 'Open Tab' button uses a hardcoded port 9876 for a local HTTP server. Two bugs: (1) The EADDRINUSE handler re-listens on port 0 (random port) but the test still asserts the page URL contains port 9876 — meaning when port 9876 is taken by a parallel test, the URL assertion checks a page served by that OTHER test, not the homepageServer. (2) `homepageServer.close()` in the finally block is fire-and-forget (no callback/promise), so the server stays half-open after the test.
acceptanceCriteria: The homepageServer uses a dynamic port (not hardcoded 9876) so it never collides with parallel tests
`homepageServer.close()` is called with a callback or wrapped in a Promise so the test waits for the server to fully close before the finally block exits
The URL assertion checks the ACTUAL port the homepageServer bound to, not a hardcoded 9876

userStories.title: Fix page and slow-mode leaks in stress-mcp-dispatch and progress tests
userStories.description: Two test files have cleanup gaps. (1) stress-mcp-dispatch.e2e.ts: tests call `setupToolTest` (returns a page) and then open a second page via `openTestAppTab`, but neither page is wrapped in try/finally — any assertion failure leaves both pages open. (2) progress.e2e.ts: the timeout test calls `testServer.setSlow(35_000)` but `testServer.setSlow(0)` to reset slow mode is called after assertions that can throw — if an elapsed-time bound assertion fails, the test server is left in 35-second slow mode for subsequent cleanup.
acceptanceCriteria: In e2e/stress-mcp-dispatch.e2e.ts, all four tests wrap their page handles in try/finally blocks so pages are closed on failure paths
In e2e/progress.e2e.ts, `testServer.setSlow(0)` is called in a finally block so slow mode is always reset regardless of assertion outcomes
